name: wat321
description: Willy's AI Tools - Real-time Claude & Codex usage widgets

wat321.enableClaude.markdownDescription: Enable Claude widgets (usage bars and session tokens). Hidden automatically if the Claude CLI is not installed.

wat321.experimental.forceClaudeAutoCompa.markdownDescription: **Experimental.** Arms Claude's built-in Auto-Compact so it triggers on your next message prompt. Disarms automatically once your prompt lands, or after 30 seconds if no prompt is sent. Can also be cancelled manually. Produces a higher-quality compaction result than the manual `/compact` command, preserving tool results and reasoning. This is the only WAT321 tool that writes outside of `~/.wat321/`. Your Claude settings are restored automatically. *Useful for long sessions when performance degrades.*

wat321.enableCodex.markdownDescription: Enable Codex widgets (usage bars and session tokens). Hidden automatically if the Codex CLI is not installed.

enumDescriptions: Automatic - Full when only one provider is active, Compact when both are active
Compact progress bars (5 blocks), slim session tokens
Text-only status bar, progress bars move to tooltips on hover
wat321.displayMode.markdownDescription: Status bar display density. Auto adjusts based on how many providers are active.

wat321.statusBarPriority.markdownDescription: Base priority for WAT321 status bar items. Higher values appear further left. Adjust if widgets overlap with other extensions. *Requires window reload.*

wat321.enableHeatmap.markdownDescription: Enable heatmap colors on the usage progress bar. The bars gradually shift through warning colors as you get closer to your limits so tight sessions stand out at a glance.

wat321.clearAllData.markdownDescription: **Reset WAT321** - Restore WAT321 to its defaults and clear everything it has stored locally. If any WAT321 tool appears unresponsive, this will reset all to a known-good state. A confirmation dialog will appear before resetting.

scripts.build: npm run clean && npm run lint && tsc
scripts.package: npm run build && npx vsce package
